evals.name: untestable-notification-service-with-integration-signals
evals.prompt: Write tests for the NotificationService in skills/testing/evals/files/eval1_notification_service.py. I need coverage for send, quiet hours deferral, and flush_deferred.
evals.expected_output: Identifies multiple testability barriers (singleton, Redis in constructor, SMTP/HTTP calls inside private methods, module-level mutable _sent_log) and suggests refactoring. Also detects integration test signals (Redis, external APIs). If tests are written, they use dependency injection and factory functions.
expectations: Performs a testability assessment before writing any tests
Identifies the singleton pattern (get_instance / _instance) as a testability barrier causing shared state across tests
Identifies that Redis is instantiated directly in __init__ (hidden dependency) and suggests injecting a storage interface
Identifies that _send_email imports and uses smtplib.SMTP directly — a side effect mixed with logic that should be behind an abstraction
Identifies the module-level _sent_log as shared mutable global state that causes test interference
Assesses test level and explicitly tells the user that Redis, SMTP, Twilio, and FCM integrations mean unit tests alone are insufficient
Suggests separating the pure quiet-hours logic (_is_quiet_hours) from the I/O-dependent send method so it can be unit tested independently
If tests are written, uses factory functions for notification request data rather than inline dicts
anti_expectations: Does not write tests that mock every Redis call and SMTP interaction without first flagging the testability problems
Does not proceed to write heavily mocked tests without suggesting refactoring the singleton and hidden dependencies
Does not ignore the _sent_log global mutable state as acceptable test design
Does not test only the happy path while skipping error cases (missing email, missing phone, unknown channel)

evals.name: behavior-focused-inventory-tests
evals.prompt: Add tests for the InventoryManager class in skills/testing/evals/files/eval2_inventory.py. Cover reserve, restock, get_status, and discontinue.
evals.expected_output: Recognizes the code is well-structured with injected storage dependency. Writes behavior-focused tests using Arrange-Act-Assert, factory functions for Product data, covers happy paths, edge cases (zero stock, max capacity cap, discontinued products), and error cases (unknown SKU, negative quantity).
expectations: Performs a testability assessment and notes the code is well-structured with an injectable storage dependency
Creates a factory function (e.g., make_product) that returns complete Product instances with sensible defaults and partial overrides
Tests are named by behavior (e.g., 'reserve reduces available quantity', 'restock caps at max quantity') not by implementation
Tests follow Arrange-Act-Assert structure with clear separation of phases
Covers reserve edge cases: reserving exact remaining quantity, reserving more than available (InsufficientStockError), reserving from discontinued product
Covers restock edge case: restocking beyond max_quantity is capped
Covers get_status transitions: in_stock, low_stock (at threshold), out_of_stock (zero), discontinued
Uses a fake or simple stub for the storage dependency rather than deep MagicMock chains
anti_expectations: Does not assert on storage.update_quantity call arguments as the primary assertion — asserts on the returned dict values instead
Does not create a 1:1 test file mapping (one test class per method) instead of organizing by behavior
Does not skip error path tests (unknown SKU raising KeyError, negative quantity raising ValueError)
Does not share mutable Product instances across test methods

evals.name: detect-shared-state-and-implementation-coupling
evals.prompt: Review the tests in skills/testing/evals/files/eval3_fragile_tests.py. These tests pass individually but fail intermittently in CI. What's wrong and how should they be fixed?
evals.expected_output: Identifies shared mutable state (shared_items list mutated by test_empty_cart), tests that assert on mock interactions instead of computed values, tests that mirror if-else code structure rather than describing behaviors, and test names that describe code paths not user-facing outcomes.
expectations: Identifies shared_items as module-level shared mutable state and pinpoints test_empty_cart calling shared_items.clear() as the cause of intermittent CI failures

Identifies that test_apply_discounts_calls_get_tier, test_apply_discounts_calls_validate_when_promo_code_given, and test_apply_discounts_does_not_call_validate_without_promo assert on mock interactions rather than business outcomes
Identifies that tests like test_gold_tier_path and test_percent_promo_path mirror the internal if-else structure rather than testing user-facing behavior
Notes that test_gold_tier_path only checks discount_source but never verifies the actual discount amount or final_total — the most important business values
Suggests using factory functions to create fresh CartItem lists per test instead of sharing a module-level list
Suggests rewriting tests to assert on final_total and discount_applied values as primary assertions
Suggests behavior-oriented test names like 'gold members get 10 percent off subtotal' or 'larger of loyalty and promo discount is applied'
anti_expectations: Does not approve the existing tests as adequate or well-written
Does not suggest adding more mock-call-assertion tests in the same pattern
Does not miss the shared_items.clear() mutation as the root cause of the intermittent failures
